diagrams.title: Three-Tier Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the three layers of client-server architecture: Presentation, Application, and Data layers with arrows indicating data flow between them
diagrams.purpose: Understanding separation of concerns and layer interactions

diagrams.title: Vertical vs Horizontal Scaling
diagrams.description: Two diagrams side-by-side showing vertical scaling (bigger single server) and horizontal scaling (multiple smaller servers)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing different approaches to handling increased load

diagrams.title: Load Balancing Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows incoming traffic being distributed by load balancer to multiple application servers, which connect to database
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how load balancing improves performance and reliability

diagrams.title: CAP Theorem Triangle
diagrams.description: A triangle diagram showing Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance at vertices with system types at edges
diagrams.purpose: Understanding trade-offs in distributed system design

diagrams.title: Cloud vs On-Premise Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing capital and operational costs over time for both deployment models
elements: On-Premise: High CapEx, Low OpEx curve
Cloud: Low CapEx, Higher OpEx curve
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing cost differences between deployment models
